sanskrit:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत।
सर्वभूतानि संमोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप॥
transliteration:
icchā-dveṣa-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni saṃmohaṃ sarge yānti parantapa
literal_meaning: All beings, O Bharata, O scorcher of foes, enter creation already bewildered by the delusion of duality born of desire and aversion.
contextual_meaning: Every psyche is born into a trance of opposites—wanting and rejecting—that obscures reality and fuels the sense of separate self.
psychological_state: Pre-egoic trance dominated by splitting (good/bad, wanted/feared) and addictive oscillation between craving and hatred.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media: loving the lifestyle you don’t have, hating the one you do, unable to stop because each swipe re-triggers the same loop of desire and aversion.
anchor_text: Notice the push-pull of liking and disliking; it blinds you to oneness.
